---
title: Column Space
description: The span of a matrix's columns.
categories:
- Linear Algebra
---

For an $m \times n$ matrix $A$, the **column space** $\operatorname{col}(A) \subseteq \mathbb{R}^m$ is the span of the columns of $A$.

**Example.**

$$
\operatorname{col}\begin{pmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 \end{pmatrix} = \operatorname{span}\{(1,1), (1,-1)\} = \{ (a+b,\ a-b) \mid a, b \in \mathbb{R} \} = \mathbb{R}^2
$$

since any $(x, y)$ can be written as $a(1,1) + b(1,-1)$ with $a = \tfrac{x+y}{2}$ and $b = \tfrac{x-y}{2}$.
